_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-national cron)
_meta.note: National batch — finishes CO federal House (Crank CO-5 R, Crow CO-6 D, Pettersen CO-7 D, Evans CO-8 R) and opens CT (Larson CT-1 D). Top-priority slug mike-thompson (CA-04 D) skipped again — 3 separate records share that slug (CA federal D, MS state R, KS state R) and the engine collapses by_slug. Federal-tier evidence: congress.gov/clerk.house.gov roll calls (119th + 118th Congress), Heritage Action scorecards, SBA Pro-Life, NRA-PVF, Giffords, Planned Parenthood / Reproductive Freedom For All, HRC, Ballotpedia, official House sites + press releases, local CO/CT press.

profile.religion: Christian (campaign + family bio frames faith as central; specific denomination not publicly self-identified); married to Lisa, 3 children
profile.background: U.S. Representative, Colorado-05 (Colorado Springs / El Paso County), freshman sworn Jan 3, 2025. Won the 2024 open-seat race after Doug Lamborn retired. Former Heritage Foundation regional director and Americans for Prosperity Colorado director. Filed for 2026 re-election; qualified by petition for the 6/30/2026 R primary and is essentially unopposed; received Trump endorsement (Aug 2025) and Colorado Springs Gazette endorsement (6/5/2026). National Democrats added CO-5 to the 2026 target list.

jeff-crank.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Heritage Action 119th Congress: 98% (session + lifetime). SBA Pro-Life America 119th: A+ (first year). NRA-PVF: AQ endorsement (2024). Strong HARD pro-life: cosponsor of HR722 Life at Conception Act and YES on OBBBA reconciliation (HR1, 7/3/2025) which contains Planned Parenthood defunding via Medicaid carveout. Biblical-marriage / family alignment: YES on HR28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (RC #12, 1/14/2025) and YES on the gender-ideology / parental-consent education bill (217-198 House). Fiscal/constitutional: cosponsor of HJ Res 12 term-limits constitutional amendment. 2A: NRA-PVF AQ endorsement; past president of Pikes Peak Firearms Coalition; sponsor of the Armed Forces Carry Rights Protection Act (concealed-carry on DoD property). Border: YES on HR29 Laken Riley Act (RC #23, 1/7/2025); YES on OBBBA border-wall + ICE funding. Election integrity: YES on HR22 SAVE Act (April 2025) — documentary proof of citizenship for federal voter registration. No cross-party deviations identified in 119th record — uniformly aligned with conservative rubric.

0.note: Cosponsor of HR722 Life at Conception Act (119th) — affirms personhood from conception.

1.note: SBA Pro-Life America A+ for 119th; YES on OBBBA reconciliation containing the Planned Parenthood defund Medicaid carveout.

2.note: Life at Conception Act cosponsorship protects embryos from discard/experimentation.

4.note: SBA Pro-Life-endorsed 2024; SBA A+ 119th — has not accepted PP/NARAL/abortion-industry funding.

2.note: Voted YES on HR28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (RC #12, 1/14/2025) — affirms biological sex as immutable in athletics.

4.note: Voted YES on the gender-ideology / parental-consent education bill ('Stopping Indoctrination and Protecting Kids') — opposes promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in K-12 schools.

1.note: YES on gender-ideology / parental-consent education bill — supports parental notification/consent on gender-related school issues.

2.note: YES on parental-consent bill applauding dismantling of Dept. of Education — opposes SOGI / gender-ideology curricula in K-12.

1.note: Voted YES on HR22 SAVE Act (April 2025) — documentary proof of citizenship for federal voter registration.
Voted YES on HR29 Laken Riley Act (RC #23, 1/7/2025) — mandatory ICE detention/deportation for arrestees.

0.note: NRA-PVF AQ endorsement (2024); past president Pikes Peak Firearms Coalition — supports constitutional-carry posture.

1.note: NRA-PVF AQ endorsement; opposes red-flag/AWB/magazine-limit framework.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on a publicly self-identified religious affiliation; profiles cite Army Ranger / Bronze Star background but not denomination
profile.background: U.S. Representative, Colorado-06 (Aurora and east-Denver suburbs), serving since Jan 3, 2019 (4th term in 119th Congress). Former Army Ranger (Bronze Star). House Intelligence Committee member; House Armed Services Committee. Filed for 2026 re-election (not running for higher office); raised $750K+ in Q4 2025; D primary 6/30/2026; general 11/3/2026. Cook PVI D+11.

jason-crow.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Heritage Action lifetime 2% (118th: 5%; 116th + 117th: 0%) — among the most reliably progressive House Democrats despite Ranger background and 'moderate' framing. Marquee abortion record: voted YES + original cosponsor of Women's Health Protection Act (federal Roe codification); led letter urging Senate to end filibuster to codify abortion; Planned Parenthood Action Fund endorsement (2020, 2024, 2026 cycles). Marquee LGBTQ record: voted YES + cosponsor of Respect for Marriage Act (HR8404, 117th, RC 373, 7/19/2022); HRC 100/100 (117th Congress). 2A: Giffords-endorsed, Vice Chair of House Gun Violence Prevention Task Force; NRA-PVF F; cosponsor + YES on HR8 Bipartisan Background Checks Act; YES on Bipartisan Safer Communities Act (incl. red-flag grants). Border: NO on HR2 Secure the Border Act (118th, RC 209, 5/11/2023); NO on HR29 Laken Riley Act (119th, 1/7/2025) — confirmed lockstep with CO D delegation despite 46 House Dem crossovers nationally. Fiscal: YES on Inflation Reduction Act ($740B reconciliation); NO on Limit Save Grow Act (118th GOP debt-ceiling/spending-cut bill). Election integrity: cosponsor + YES + floor leader on HR1 For the People Act (federal election takeover). No cross-party deviations on rubric cells identified — moderate in tone, party-line in voting record.

0.note: Voted YES on Women's Health Protection Act; original cosponsor (116th & 117th) — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Led letter urging Senate to end filibuster to codify abortion; SBA Pro-Life: 'consistently voted against efforts to protect the lives of the unborn.'

4.note: Planned Parenthood Action Fund endorsement (2020, 2024, 2026) — does not meet 'never accepted abortion-industry PAC funding' bar.

0.note: Voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act (HR8404, RC 373, 7/19/2022) — codifies same-sex marriage federally.

1.note: YES on RFMA — opposes natural-marriage-only framework.

4.note: HRC 100/100 (117th) — affirms LGBTQ promotion in federal policy.

1.note: HRC 100/100 indicates support for SOGI mandates / Equality Act framework — limits conscience exemptions for Christian medical professionals, business owners, adoption agencies.

2.note: YES on Inflation Reduction Act ($740B reconciliation); NO on Limit Save Grow Act (118th GOP debt-ceiling/spending-cut bill); Heritage Action 5% (118th) — opposite of deficit-discipline rubric.

1.note: Cosponsor + floor leader on HR1 For the People Act (federal takeover of election admin) — opposite of photo-ID/citizenship-verification posture.
NO on HR2 Secure the Border Act (118th, RC 209, 5/11/2023); NO on HR29 Laken Riley Act (119th, 1/7/2025) — opposite of mandatory deportation.

0.note: Giffords-endorsed; Vice Chair of House Gun Violence Prevention Task Force; NRA-PVF F — opposite of constitutional-carry posture.

1.note: Cosponsor + YES on HR8 Bipartisan Background Checks Act; YES on Bipartisan Safer Communities Act (incl. red-flag grants); platform supports AWB + high-capacity-mag ban.

2.note: Supports AWB, mag-limit, bump-stock ban; NRA-PVF F — opposite of NFA/GCA repeal posture.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on a publicly self-identified religious affiliation; public bio emphasizes family-recovery/addiction story (her mother's opioid recovery), not faith
profile.background: U.S. Representative, Colorado-07 (Lakewood / mountain communities), serving since Jan 3, 2023 (2nd term in 119th Congress). Held campaign kickoff Saturday June 13, 2026 in Salida; raised $270K with $900K+ COH as of April 2026. House Education and the Workforce Committee. Defining advocacy issue is addiction/recovery (her mother's opioid addiction). Filed for 2026 re-election; D primary 6/30/2026; general 11/3/2026.

brittany-pettersen.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Heritage Action 5% (118th Congress) — extreme low, consistent with reliable progressive voting across most categories. Marquee abortion record: Reproductive Freedom for All (NARAL) endorsement; Planned Parenthood Action Fund endorsement for 2026 cycle; cosponsor of HR12 Women's Health Protection Act of 2025 (119th); as CO state senator lead-cosponsored the Reproductive Health Equity Act codifying CO abortion right. Marquee 2A record: Giffords Law Center endorsement; member of House Gun Violence Prevention Task Force; cosponsored federal Assault Weapons Ban + bump-stock-loophole closure + expanded universal background checks; cosponsor 'Recognizing Gun Violence as a Public Health Emergency Act.' Border: NO on HR29 / S5 Laken Riley Act (119th Congress, RC 6 + 23, Jan 2025) — confirmed lockstep with CO D delegation. HRC YEO listed (LGBTQ-allied federal official). ★ Cross-party deviation: cosponsor of HR27 HALT Fentanyl Act (119th) and cross-party SUPPORT for Patients & Communities Reauthorization Act 2025 (with Brett Guthrie R-KY) and Communities of Recovery Reauthorization Act 2023 (with Zach Nunn R-IA) — driven by her personal addiction-recovery story; conservative-aligned on fentanyl scheduling but no rubric cell maps cleanly to drug-scheduling so noted here, not scored.

0.note: Cosponsor of HR12 Women's Health Protection Act of 2025 (codifies abortion right) — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Reproductive Freedom for All (NARAL) endorsement; WHPA cosponsor — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: NARAL/Reproductive Freedom for All + Planned Parenthood Action Fund endorsements (2026 cycle) — does not meet 'never accepted abortion-industry PAC funding' bar.
HRC YEO listing (Your Elected Officials — affirmed LGBTQ-allied) — affirms LGBTQ promotion in federal policy.

1.note: NO on HR29 / S5 Laken Riley Act (119th, RC 6 Jan 7 2025 + RC 23 Jan 22 2025) — opposite of mandatory deportation.

0.note: Giffords Law Center endorsement; House Gun Violence Prevention Task Force member — opposite of constitutional-carry posture.

1.note: Cosponsored federal Assault Weapons Ban, bump-stock-loophole closure, expanded universal background checks; cosponsor 'Recognizing Gun Violence as a Public Health Emergency Act.'

2.note: Cosponsor of federal AWB + universal background checks; as CO state senator sponsored CO Extreme Risk Protection Order (red-flag) bill — opposite of NFA/GCA repeal posture.

profile.religion: Christian (homeschooler; cites Heritage Defense — Christian homeschoolers' legal defense organization — as a self-listed affiliation)
profile.background: U.S. Representative, Colorado-08 (north Denver suburbs / Adams + Weld counties), freshman sworn Jan 3, 2025. Flipped the seat from Yadira Caraveo (D) in 2024. Former Army officer / Black Hawk pilot and former Arvada police officer. CO-8 is a top-target swing district. Filed for 2026 re-election; faces R primary 6/30/2026 against Adam DeRito and 8 other challengers. Member of Republican Governance Group + Main Street Caucus + Problem Solvers Caucus (moderate-business R wing). Heritage Action 119th: 74-75%.

gabe-evans.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] HEAVY CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS for an R — Evans is a moderate-business swing-district freshman, not a Freedom Caucus profile. PRO-LIFE WITH EXCEPTIONS: self-identifies pro-life with rape/incest/life-of-mother exceptions; OPPOSES a nationwide abortion ban ('issue should be left to states'); was notably absent from the PP defund amendment markup in reconciliation committee; voted YES on OBBBA final passage (which included PP defunding). ★★ MARRIAGE: SUPPORTS the Respect for Marriage Act; supports CO Amendment J (strips same-sex marriage ban from CO constitution); was 'excused' / did not vote on CO SCR24-003 (referring same-sex marriage to ballot) — major FALSE on biblical_marriage. ★★ BORDER: cosponsored American Families United Act (immigration-process reform) and 2026 DIGNIDAD Act (pathway to legal status for up to 12 million illegal immigrants paired with enforcement); says DACA program should remain in place — major FALSE on the 'mandatory deportation' rubric cell despite YES on Laken Riley + OBBBA border funding. CONSERVATIVE-ALIGNED CORE: opposed CO state gun bills (red-flag, AWB, age-increase); additional sponsor of HR38 Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act of 2025; signed CO Libertarian Party pledge to 'move to abolish all unconstitutional gun laws including the NFA.' Voted YES on HR22 SAVE Act (RC #102, 4/10/2025). FIRE Act (HR6387) sponsor limiting EPA air-quality regulations. Public-safety / former-cop background drives 'back-the-blue' framing; no rubric cell in this 10-category federal set maps to police-support, so noted here, not scored.

1.note: YES on OBBBA final passage (HR1, 7/3/2025) — contained PP defunding via Medicaid carveout.

4.note: Endorsed by anti-abortion lawmakers (Speaker Johnson, Cotton, CO Sen. Kirkmeyer); no PP/NARAL endorsements documented.

1.note: ★ Cross-party deviation: PUBLICLY supports the Respect for Marriage Act and supports CO Amendment J stripping the same-sex marriage ban from CO constitution — opposite of opposing same-sex marriage in law.

2.note: Opposed CO bill requiring schools to call students by chosen names and new buildings to have all-gender restrooms — affirms biological sex framing.
Opposed CO chosen-names / all-gender-restrooms school bill — opposes SOGI / gender-ideology curricula in K-12.

1.note: Voted YES on HR22 SAVE Act (RC #102, 4/10/2025) — documentary proof of citizenship for voter registration.

★★ Cross-party deviation: cosponsored 2026 DIGNIDAD Act (pathway to legal status for up to 12 million illegal immigrants); cosponsored American Families United Act; says DACA program should remain in place — opposite of mandatory deportation. Did vote YES on Laken Riley Act but the rubric question (mandatory deportation of ALL illegal aliens incl. minors) is contradicted by the amnesty-cosponsor evidence.

0.note: Additional sponsor of HR38 Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act of 2025; cosponsored 2023 CO concealed-carry constitutional amendment — supports constitutional carry.

1.note: Opposed CO gun bills (SB23-170 red flag, SB23-169 age-increase, HB23-1230 AWB); advocated arming teachers — opposes red-flag, AWB, mag-limit framework.

2.note: Signed CO Libertarian Party candidate pledge to 'move to abolish all unconstitutional gun laws, including the NFA' — supports NFA/GCA repeal.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (publicly identified; long-time CT Catholic Democrat)
profile.background: U.S. Representative, Connecticut-01 (Hartford-area), serving since Jan 3, 1999 (13+ terms; longest-serving member of CT House delegation). Ranking member, House Social Security Subcommittee; lead sponsor of Social Security 2100 Act. Suffered complex partial seizure on House floor Feb 11, 2025 (returned to votes Feb 13). RUNNING FOR RE-ELECTION 2026 — but LOST CT Democratic Party endorsement to Luke Bronin at state convention May 11, 2026; continuing to contested D primary 8/11/2026.

john-larson.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Heritage Action 119th Congress: 0%. CAGW lifetime: 9%. Self-declared 100% Planned Parenthood / 100% Reproductive Freedom For All (on his official House issues page). Senior coastal-Democrat profile: NO on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act both 118th (HR26, RC 029, 1/11/2023) and 119th (HR21, RC 027, 1/23/2025); NO on H.Con.Res. 3 (condemning attacks on pro-life facilities AND CHURCHES, RC 030, 1/11/2023); NO on HR734 + HR28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports (both Congresses). YES on Respect for Marriage Act and YES on Equality Act (HR5, 117th, RC 072, 2021). Fiscal: NO on HR23 Family/Small Business Taxpayer Protection (rescind IRS funding); NO on HR2811 Limit Save Grow Act; NO on HR3746 Fiscal Responsibility Act; NO on H.Con.Res. 14 (119th GOP budget); NO on HR1919 Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act (RC 201). Border: NO on S5 Laken Riley Act (RC 023, 1/7/2025); NO on HR30 Preventing Violence Against Women by Illegal Aliens Act (RC 017, 1/8/2025); NO on HR2494 POLICE Act (118th, RC 225). 2A: NO on HR3091 + HR2255 Federal LEO Service Weapon Purchase Act (both Congresses); his official Issues page features 'Gun Violence Prevention'; NRA-PVF F. Election integrity: NO on HR22 SAVE Act (RC 102, 4/10/2025); cosponsor of John R.
